image_prompts.prompt: Hook visual: What if you could edit a single letter in your DNA without a double cut?
Context visual: Introducing base editing—a genius tool that rewrites genetic code, one letter at a time.
Escalation visual: Unlike classic CRISPR, which cuts both DNA strands, base editing avoids that damage.
Evidence visual: Developed in the Liu lab, this method shows promise in precise gene therapy, still in trial stages.
Payoff visual: Imagine curing genetic diseases by simply changing one DNA letter.
Cta visual: Curious about the future of gene editing? Hit that follow button for more!
